qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Browser bundle relative partials escape the configured root and read same-origin resources
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets browser-bundle relative partial resolution through `src/build/fs-impl-browser.ts` plus the loader's default `contains()` fallback, while the advisory describes Node-side absolute-path access through `fs.fallback()`/`require.resolve()` in `src/fs/loader.ts` without root enforcement.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause: unrestricted Node `require.resolve()` fallback handling of absolute include/layout/render paths.
evidence: The advisory states the root cause is `require.resolve()` fallback resolving absolute paths outside the configured directories, and PR #855 adds root enforcement specifically around `fs.fallback(file)` in `src/fs/loader.ts`.

The only final qualifying finding describes a browser-only relative `../` partial escape through `src/build/fs-impl-browser.ts`, where the browser adapter lacks `contains()` and same-origin XHR reads an out-of-root resource.

The finding's own reproduction distinguishes the bug from the advisory path: it prints `EVOHUNT_BROWSER_ROOT_ESCAPE_OK ...` for the browser UMD build and `EVOHUNT_NODE_CONTROL_BLOCKED ... Failed to lookup "../secrets/secret.txt" ...` for the Node build.
